title: Vaccinium floccosum
intro: Vaccinium floccosum, also known as the woolly blueberry, is a type of shrub native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against oxidative stress, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Vaccinium floccosum include anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Vaccinium floccosum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to support cardiovascular health and immune function.
taxonomy: Vaccinium floccosum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. This family, commonly known as the heath family, consists of plants that are typically found in acidic and often moorland or heathland habitats. The genus Vaccinium includes various species of shrubs and small trees that produce edible fruits, such as blueberries and cranberries. Vaccinium floccosum is native to China and is one of the many species within the Vaccinium genus. Within the plant kingdom, Vaccinium floccosum is further classified as a member of the kingdom Plantae.
